name: io.github.dtkmn/mcp-zap-server
title: MCP ZAP Server
description: Safe, self-hosted OWASP ZAP operator for guided AI security scans and reports.

runtimeArguments.name: --network
runtimeArguments.description: Docker network containing the separately running OWASP ZAP daemon.

runtimeArguments.name: --user
runtimeArguments.description: Run with the standard zaproxy/zap-stable UID/GID so shared report workspace files remain writable by both containers.

runtimeArguments.name: -p
runtimeArguments.description: Expose the streamable HTTP MCP endpoint on localhost.

runtimeArguments.name: -v
runtimeArguments.description: Named report workspace volume. The external OWASP ZAP container must mount the same volume at /zap/wrk.

environmentVariables.name: ZAP_API_URL
environmentVariables.description: Hostname or URL of a separately running OWASP ZAP daemon reachable from this container.

environmentVariables.name: ZAP_API_KEY
environmentVariables.description: API key configured on the OWASP ZAP daemon.

environmentVariables.name: MCP_API_KEY
environmentVariables.description: API key clients must send as X-API-Key.

environmentVariables.name: MCP_SERVER_TOOLS_SURFACE
environmentVariables.description: Tool surface to expose. Use guided for the safer default workflow, including report readback. Use expert only when clients need raw ZAP tools outside the guided surface.

headers.name: X-API-Key
headers.description: MCP API key configured with MCP_API_KEY.

environmentVariables.name: ZAP_API_KEY
environmentVariables.description: API key configured on the OWASP ZAP daemon.

environmentVariables.name: MCP_SERVER_TOOLS_SURFACE

io.modelcontextprotocol.registry/publish.installation: Docker Compose remains the easiest install path. OCI package installs require a separately running OWASP ZAP daemon reachable from the MCP container.
io.modelcontextprotocol.registry/publish.externalDependency: OWASP ZAP is not bundled in the MCP server image. Start ZAP separately on the configured Docker network, initialize mcp-zap-wrk for UID/GID 1000:1000, and mount the same mcp-zap-wrk:/zap/wrk volume into ZAP so reports generated by ZAP are readable by MCP.
